queries.sql: SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '2 minutes') AS _time, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM "{stream}" GROUP BY _time ORDER BY _time ASC
SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '1 minute') AS ts_minute, operation_mode, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM "{stream}" GROUP BY ts_minute, operation_mode ORDER BY ts_minute ASC, operation_mode ASC
SELECT _timestamp, pallet_id, facility_zone, log FROM "{stream}" ORDER BY _timestamp DESC LIMIT 5

SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '5 minutes') AS _time, SUM(throughput_rate) AS total_tput, MIN(throughput_rate) AS min_tput, MAX(throughput_rate) AS max_tput FROM "{stream}" WHERE throughput_rate IS NOT NULL GROUP BY _time ORDER BY _time ASC

SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '1 minute') AS minute_bucket, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE operation_mode='auto') AS auto_cnt, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE operation_mode='manual') AS manual_cnt, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE operation_mode='hybrid') AS hybrid_cnt FROM "{stream}" GROUP BY minute_bucket ORDER BY minute_bucket ASC

expected.note: 1-min histogram with 3 FILTER-counts as pivot per bucket

queries.sql: SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '30 seconds') AS half_min, facility_zone, COUNT(*) AS cnt, ROUND(AVG(load_factor), 2) AS avg_load, ROUND(MIN(charge_remaining), 2) AS min_charge FROM "{stream}" WHERE cell_temp >= 22.0 GROUP BY half_min, facility_zone HAVING ROUND(AVG(load_factor), 2) > 30.0 ORDER BY half_min ASC, facility_zone ASC

WITH bucketed AS (SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '2 minutes') AS block, COUNT(*) AS cnt, ROUND(AVG(throughput_rate), 2) AS avg_tput FROM "{stream}" GROUP BY block) SELECT *, ROUND(AVG(avg_tput) OVER (ORDER BY block ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW), 2) AS smoothed_avg, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS peak_rank FROM bucketed ORDER BY block ASC

expected.note: CTE with histogram + window over histogram buckets

queries.sql: SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '5 minutes') AS window_5m, sorter_model, COUNT(*) AS samples, ROUND(AVG(throughput_rate), 2) AS avg_tput, ROUND(AVG(cell_temp), 2) AS avg_temp, ROUND(CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN scan_attempts >= 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100.0, 2) AS high_scan_pct FROM "{stream}" WHERE throughput_rate IS NOT NULL GROUP BY window_5m, sorter_model HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY window_5m ASC, avg_tput DESC

expected.note: 5-min histogram + sorter breakdown + computed high_scan_pct, HAVING >=2

queries.sql: SELECT histogram(_timestamp) AS default_bucket, control_center, operation_mode, COUNT(*) AS event_count, ROUND(SUM(item_count), 0) AS total_items, ROUND(AVG(charge_remaining), 2) AS avg_charge FROM "{stream}" WHERE item_count >= 100 GROUP BY default_bucket, control_center, operation_mode ORDER BY default_bucket ASC, event_count DESC

expected.note: Default histogram + 3-field GROUP BY with item filter

queries.sql: SELECT bucket_a._time AS early_window, bucket_a.cnt AS early_cnt, bucket_a.avg_tput AS early_avg_tput, bucket_b._time AS late_window, bucket_b.cnt AS late_cnt, bucket_b.avg_tput AS late_avg_tput FROM (SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '3 minutes') AS _time, COUNT(*) AS cnt, ROUND(AVG(throughput_rate), 2) AS avg_tput FROM "{stream}" WHERE _timestamp < (SELECT MIN(_timestamp) + (MAX(_timestamp) - MIN(_timestamp)) / 2 FROM "{stream}") GROUP BY _time) bucket_a FULL OUTER JOIN (SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '3 minutes') AS _time, COUNT(*) AS cnt, ROUND(AVG(throughput_rate), 2) AS avg_tput FROM "{stream}" WHERE _timestamp >= (SELECT MIN(_timestamp) + (MAX(_timestamp) - MIN(_timestamp)) / 2 FROM "{stream}") GROUP BY _time) bucket_b ON bucket_a._time = bucket_b._time ORDER BY early_window ASC NULLS LAST

expected.note: FULL OUTER JOIN of two histograms splitting at mid-point timestamp

queries.sql: SELECT histogram(_timestamp, '1 minute') AS minute_slot, COUNT(*) AS total_events, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN cell_temp >= 28.0 THEN throughput_rate ELSE NULL END), 2) AS avg_tput_hot, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN cell_temp < 28.0 THEN throughput_rate ELSE NULL END), 2) AS avg_tput_cool FROM "{stream}" WHERE throughput_rate IS NOT NULL GROUP BY minute_slot ORDER BY minute_slot ASC

expected.note: 1-min histogram with conditional AVGs (hot vs cool temp split using CASE)
